user.name: Nischal
user.bio: Ceiling chaser. Differential addict. Fully willing to captain chaos if the upside is there.
user.summary: Survived on one differential masterstroke yesterday.

friend.name: Shreyas
friend.bio: The steady hand in the rivalry. Likes core picks, safe routes, and quietly collecting points.
friend.summary: Stayed close by backing the safer core.

posts.title: Sky captaincy could break the template
posts.summary: Backed Suryakumar for pure upside and leaned into a Mumbai-heavy core to try stretching the overall lead.
posts.content: This was one of those nights where safety felt like surrender. I knew Shreyas would sit on the reliable captain route, so I leaned into the volatility and gave the armband to Sky. If Mumbai's middle order clicks, the lead grows and the banter gets unbearable.

posts.title: Template trust versus ceiling chase
posts.summary: Played it steady with Ruturaj captain and trusted the reliable core to punish an over-aggressive opponent.
posts.content: The whole point of a rivalry is forcing the other person to overextend. Nischal has chased the explosion, and I am happy sitting on strong, repeatable points. If Ruturaj does his job and the all-rounders chip in, this gets very uncomfortable for him very quickly.

posts.title: Differential rescue mission worked
posts.summary: A late bowling differential flipped a rough night into a narrow win and gave the rivalry a little extra heat.
posts.content: This matchup looked cooked for most of the night. Then one differential bowler ripped through the order, and suddenly the entire week changed. These are the nights that make fantasy feel worth the stress.

posts.title: Patience finally paid off
posts.summary: Stayed disciplined, avoided the obvious trap pick, and let the all-rounders quietly do the damage.
posts.content: Not every win needs a miracle. Sometimes the correct move is refusing the flashy option and letting structure beat adrenaline. This one was a reminder that calm lineups can still land a punch.

publishing.method: Only signed-in owners can use the dashboard. Public visitors can read the posts but cannot create or edit anything.
